title: State of the software engineering job market in 2026, part 2
author: Gergely Orosz
summary: Deepdive into the tech jobs market with exclusive data revealing AI labs are more attractive than Big Tech, native mobile & frontend roles are declining, management’s “great flattening”, and more

title: Kubernetes and retiring at the top with Kelsey Hightower
author: Gergely Orosz
summary: Kelsey Hightower reflects on his journey from self-taught technician to Google Distinguished Engineer, sharing lessons on open source, Kubernetes, AI, and building technology that serves people.

title: Ideas: slow down to speed up when working with AI agents
author: Gergely Orosz
summary: Devs are generating twice as much code (or more) than just 6 months ago, which is a problem for quality, reliability, and tech debt. A rational fix is available for these, but who’s acting rationally?

title: The Pulse: a trend of trying to cut back on AI spend within eng departments?
author: Gergely Orosz
summary: Top-down and bottom-up efforts to rationalize AI token spend, interesting AI coding stats from Cursor, GCP suspends $2M/month customer without warning, and more

title: Building OpenCode with Dax Raad
author: Gergely Orosz
summary: Dax Raad, co-founder of OpenCode, joins the show to discuss their explosive growth, the limits of AI coding tools, and why engineering judgment still matters.

title: State of the software engineering job market in 2026
author: Gergely Orosz
summary: A deepdive into today’s tech jobs market, with exclusive data on software engineering jobs, the AI engineering boom, whether AI engineering is “replacing” software engineering hiring, and more

title: The Pulse: Antigravity 2.0 takes ‘IDE’ out of its new IDE
author: Gergely Orosz
summary: Negative feedback greets Google’s redesigned AI IDE, why Google’s product ecosystem is so chaotic, Meta cuts 10% of staff as it hits record revenue and profits, and more

title: Why Rust is different, with Alice Ryhl
author: Gergely Orosz
summary: Alice Ryhl from Google’s Android Rust team explains why developers love Rust, and what makes the language so powerful for building reliable software.

title: AI’s impact on software engineers in 2026: key trends, Part 2
author: Gergely Orosz
summary: Tradeoffs of AI tooling, why adopting AI at company-level is hard, what’s changed in two years, and more. The third and final part of a series analyzing our 2026 AI survey results

title: The Pulse: Forward deployed engineering heats up again
author: Gergely Orosz
summary: Also: a look into rising tech job losses, self-reporting 100% AI usage at big companies, vibe coding & agentic engineering merging, and more

title: TypeScript, C# and Turbo Pascal with Anders Hejlsberg
author: Gergely Orosz
summary: Anders Hejlsberg reflects on building Turbo Pascal, C#, and TypeScript, and shares what AI may change about the future of software engineering.

title: Revisiting “No Silver Bullets” in the age of AI
author: Gergely Orosz
summary: Does the noted “No Silver Bullets” paper by the author of a classic engineering book still hold up, 40 years later? Is AI the long-sought single silver bullet – or has one been around for years?

title: The Pulse: Did capacity shortages turn Anthropic hostile to devs?
author: Gergely Orosz
summary: Also: Amazon finally allows engineers to use Claude Code and Codex, Meta forcefully assigns engineers to data labelling work ahead of layoffs, more small “AI-forward” teams, and more

title: Designing Data-Intensive Applications: The Cloud & Doing the Right Thing
author: Gergely Orosz
summary: How the cloud changes the way we build applications, and why engineers’ ethical choices matter more than ever. Excerpt from the book, ‘Designing Data-Intensive Applications’, 2nd edition

title: The Pulse: AI load breaks GitHub – why not other vendors?
author: Gergely Orosz
summary: Also: Anthropic’s speed run to break devs’ goodwill, big price increases from GitHub Copilot, Mitchell Hashimoto on the “building block economy,” and more

title: Building Pi, and what makes self-modifying software so fascinating
author: Gergely Orosz
summary: Mario Zechner, creator of Pi, joins Armin Ronacher to explore AI coding’s limits, arguing that human judgment still matters most in an agent-driven world.

title: How will AI change operating systems? Part 1: Ubuntu and Linux
author: Gergely Orosz
summary: A deepdive with the Canonical team into how AI is changing Ubuntu, why they’re betting on local-first LLMs, and a look into other Linux distributions

title: The Pulse: AI token spending out of control – what’s next?
author: Gergely Orosz
summary: Details from 15 tech companies on the rapid growth of token spend, and their responses to it. Also: AI vendors can’t keep up with demand, plummeting morale at Meta, and more.

title: Designing Data-intensive Applications with Martin Kleppmann
author: Gergely Orosz
summary: Martin Kleppmann on scaling, his updated Designing Data-Intensive Applications, and what’s next for AI-era systems.

title: Learnings from conducting ~1,000 interviews at Amazon
author: Gergely Orosz
summary: Steve Huynh, formerly Principal Engineer at Amazon, shares observations from 10+ years of interviewing software engineers, and an excerpt from his new book, Technical Behavioral Interview

title: The Pulse: Did Anthropic’s new model just boost rival Codex’s market share?
author: Gergely Orosz
summary: Anthropic’s new model, Fable, has restrictions many users find unacceptable. Also: a new trend of smart model routing, Coinbase’s core service has no automatic cross-zone failover, and more.

title: Why is Meta destroying its engineering organization?
author: Gergely Orosz
summary: Leadership at the social media giant has been on an AI-fueled rampage through its engineering org. We report what’s happened

title: CI/CD with Robert Erez
author: Gergely Orosz
summary: Robert Erez of Octopus Deploy joins me to discuss Kubernetes, GitOps, progressive delivery, AI in CI/CD, and the evolving practices behind modern software delivery.

title: The Pulse: Big implications of US banning Anthropic’s new model, Fable
author: Gergely Orosz
summary: Also: a follow-up on Meta destroying its own engineering culture, the SpaceX IPO, SpaceX buys Cursor, Cursor’s GitHub competitor, and more.

title: Slow down to speed up: so much has changed in 6 months’ time
author: Gergely Orosz
summary: An overview of what’s changed in engineering during the last six months, how various tech companies are changing how they work, and why slowing down could be a sensible strategy

title: Tech interviews with NeetCode
author: Gergely Orosz
summary: NeetCode shares his journey from Amazon and Google to building a startup, and why deep expertise still matters in the age of AI.

title: Impressions from visiting OpenAI, Anthropic, & Cursor
author: Gergely Orosz
summary: A peek into where software engineering is headed from inside the sector’s leading AI labs. Agents running in the cloud are a major trend, while coding harnesses are spreading beyond the craft

title: How Kent Beck shapes the software engineering industry
author: Gergely Orosz
summary: Kent Beck reflects on Agile, TDD, and why building trust—not just generating code—will define the future of software engineering in the AI era.

title: Tech jobs market in 2026, part 3: hiring managers & job seekers
author: Gergely Orosz
summary: The market where nobody finds each other, the hottest market for AI-related positions, tough for engineering leaders, and more. Based on details from 50+ hiring managers & job seekers

title: The Pragmatic Engineer AMA
author: Gergely Orosz
summary: In this AMA episode, Gergely Orosz answers listener questions on AI, engineering, hiring, and careers.

title: The Pulse: What can we learn from Bun’s rapid Rust rewrite with AI?
author: Gergely Orosz
summary: A rewrite done in 11 days that would have taken a small team a year to complete, for $165K in tokens. Also: coding LLM “wars” heat up, AI fakers from North Korea still a problem when hiring, and more

title: What is “loop engineering?”
author: Gergely Orosz
summary: There’s talk about loop engineering, but what is it exactly? I looked into it, and found triggers, cron jobs, AI slop & more. Is it a “here today, gone tomorrow” trend?

title: Context engineering with Dex Horthy
author: Gergely Orosz
summary: Dex Horthy explains why context engineering is key to building more effective AI-assisted software without sacrificing code quality.

title: The Pulse: Grok’s CLI caught uploading all your local files to the cloud
author: Gergely Orosz
summary: Also: engineering leaders concerned about continued increase in code review load, devs at enterprises surprised by high enterprise pricing, and more

title: Pushing software engineering limits with “napkin math”
author: Gergely Orosz
summary: Turbopuffer cofounder Simon Eskildsen on the benefits of longer tenure, using first principles to build durable software – and why founders should be cautious when raising VC money
